plan: Create `src/domain/types.ts` with TypeScript interfaces: `ProbeSample` (url, statusCode, rttMs, timestampMs), `NetworkSnapshot` (samples array, aggregated stats like medianRtt, jitter, packetLoss, bandwidth, timestamp), and `SceneParams` (normalized 0-1 values: height, symmetry, fracture, fog, lightIntensity, ruinLevel)

Create `src/domain/normalize.ts` with pure normalization helpers: `normalizeRtt(rttMs: number, min?: number, max?: number): number` mapping RTT to 0-1 range, `normalizeJitter(jitterMs: number, max?: number): number`, and a general `clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number` utility. All helpers must clamp invalid values (NaN, Infinity, negatives) to safe defaults.

Create `src/domain/snapshotToScene.ts` with a pure function `mapSnapshotToScene(snapshot: NetworkSnapshot): SceneParams` that converts aggregated network metrics into normalized scene parameters. Low RTT/jitter → tall/clean/symmetrical; high latency/instability → fractured/heavy/ruinous.
Create `src/domain/index.ts` barrel file re-exporting all public types and functions from the domain module.

Create `tests/unit/domain/normalize.test.ts` with unit tests covering: normal range inputs, boundary values (0, max), clamping of negative values, clamping of NaN/Infinity, default parameter behavior.

Create `tests/unit/domain/snapshotToScene.test.ts` with unit tests covering: ideal network conditions produce clean scene params, degraded conditions produce ruinous params, edge cases with empty samples or zero values.
Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify all code passes the quality gate.
testing_recommendations: Unit test every normalization helper with edge cases: NaN, Infinity, -Infinity, negative numbers, zero, and values at/beyond boundaries
Unit test clamp utility independently with out-of-range, in-range, and boundary inputs
Unit test snapshotToScene mapping with ideal snapshot (low RTT, low jitter) asserting SceneParams reflect clean/tall cathedral
Unit test snapshotToScene mapping with degraded snapshot (high RTT, high jitter) asserting SceneParams reflect fractured/ruinous cathedral
Unit test snapshotToScene with edge-case snapshots: empty samples array, single sample, all-zero values
Verify all domain exports have no three.js imports (enforce domain purity constraint)
Run full quality gate: lint, typecheck, test, and confirm all pass before marking ticket complete
risks: Over-engineering the type definitions — keep interfaces minimal and extend later as downstream stories (US-006, US-007, US-009) reveal actual needs
Choosing normalization ranges (RTT min/max, jitter max) too narrowly or too broadly — use sensible defaults (e.g., 0-1000ms RTT) but make them configurable parameters
SceneParams fields may need to evolve as rendering stories (US-010, US-011, US-013) are implemented — design for additive extension rather than locking down now
